title: rubus × glaciacus
intro: Rubus × glaciacus, also known as Himalayan raspberry, is a hybrid of the Rubus idaeus and Rubus fruticosus species, resulting in a deciduous shrub with edible fruit and medicinal properties. The plant's berries have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to their high content of dietary fiber and anthocyanins, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rubus × glaciacus include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing relief from symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to soothe digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
